title: macroprudential policy / leveraged lending / bank regulation | Research Paper | Macroprudential Policy and the Revolving Door of Risk: Lesso...
summary: This May 2017 Federal Reserve Bank of New York staff report examines the U.S. interagency guidance on leveraged lending and finds that while the guidance reduced banks' leveraged lending activity, it primarily affected large, closely supervised banks and triggered a migration of leveraged lending to nonbank entities. The research highlights the 'revolving door of risk' phenomenon and questions whether the guidance accomplished its broader goal of reducing systemic financial risk.
